text.title: Vector DB Prod Setup — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules: durable persistence, resource limits, HA per DB class, backup + restore drilled, pre+post deploy checklist.

rule.statement: Production data MUST sit on a durable persistent volume (EBS gp3, Ceph RBD, Premium SSD). emptyDir or local-storage without replication is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Ephemeral or non-replicated storage loses data on node failure. Durable storage survives pod evictions, node drains, AZ reboots.

rule.statement: K8s Pod MUST set both `requests` and `limits` for CPU + memory. Unbounded resource leads to noisy-neighbour evictions.
rule.rationale: Without requests, the pod may not be scheduled on a node with enough memory; without limits, a misbehaving pod takes neighbours down.

rule.statement: HA strategy MUST match DB class: Milvus/Weaviate ≥3 replicas (distributed); Qdrant single replica + frequent snapshot (no native HA in OSS); pgvector PG replication.
rule.rationale: Each DB has different HA primitives. Forcing 3-replica on single-leader Qdrant doesn't add HA; it adds confusion.

rule.statement: Backup MUST run on schedule (≥daily) AND restore MUST be drilled in staging quarterly. Untested backup is no backup.
rule.rationale: Backups that never restore successfully are common. Quarterly drill verifies the chain end-to-end.

rule.statement: Each prod deploy MUST run a pre-deploy checklist (monitoring live, backup wired, security config applied) and a post-deploy verification (latency in band, ingest works, search returns results).
rule.rationale: Skipped checklists are how prod regressions ship. Codify the steps so nobody forgets one at 02:00.
